entries.title: Kurogiri Is Shirakumo Remains Restricted Knowledge

context.label: Kurogiri and Shirakumo truth is confronted

coordinates.label: Hero society in open war with PLF

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Aizawa and Present Mic confront evidence that Kurogiri was made from Oboro Shirakumo, making the Nomu truth personal and restricted.
content.injection: Treat Kurogiri/Shirakumo knowledge as sensitive. Only involved heroes, police, and investigation personnel should know it unless the story reveals it.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

content.fact: The Shirakumo reveal wounds Aizawa and Present Mic personally because Kurogiri is tied to a dead classmate rather than just another Nomu asset.
content.injection: If Aizawa or Present Mic references Kurogiri here, give the moment grief, anger, and professional restraint rather than casual exposition.

entries.title: Hawks Intel Makes The Raid Possible

context.label: Hawks intelligence points heroes toward PLF war

coordinates.label: Hero society in open war with PLF

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks’ infiltration and coded intelligence are what let heroes identify the Paranormal Liberation Front structure, hospital link, and timing for coordinated action.
content.injection: Before the raids, keep Hawks as the critical intelligence source. The heroes are acting on risky covert information, not omniscient certainty.

entries.title: Hawks Is Still Undercover Until The Raid Breaks

context.label: Hawks intelligence points heroes toward PLF war

content.fact: Hawks remains embedded near the PLF while secretly feeding heroes information, so his loyalty and movements must remain ambiguous to villains.
content.injection: Before open combat at the villa, do not have PLF members casually know Hawks is a spy unless the scene is about suspicion or exposure.

entries.title: Coordinated Raids Split The War Across Fronts

coordinates.label: Hero society in open war with PLF

content.fact: The heroes launch simultaneous operations against Jaku Hospital and Gunga Mountain Villa while evacuation teams protect civilians.
content.injection: Use the war as a split-front operation. Hospital, villa, and evacuation scenes must not collapse into one battlefield until later escalation.

content.fact: Many students begin the war in evacuation or rear support roles rather than direct frontline hero combat.
content.injection: At raid start, keep most students in support and evacuation positions. Their direct combat involvement should escalate only as the war breaks containment.

entries.title: The PLF Is Hidden From The Public Before The Raid

context.label: Hawks intelligence points heroes toward PLF war

coordinates.label: Hero society in open war with PLF

content.fact: The Paranormal Liberation Front is a massive organized threat, but its full scale and leadership are not public knowledge before the raid.
content.injection: Before the raids, civilians and ordinary students must not understand the PLF as a nationwide army unless briefed by authorities.

entries.title: Garaki Link Makes The Hospital A Strategic Target

context.label: Doctor Garaki is exposed as PLF collaborator

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Doctor Garaki’s hospital and laboratory matter because they connect Nomu production, Shigaraki enhancement, and All For One’s hidden infrastructure.
content.injection: Use Jaku Hospital as a strategic target, not a random villain base. Garaki ties medicine, Nomu science, and Shigaraki’s transformation together.

content.injection: In pre-war scenes, ordinary school rhythms can continue, but keep an undertone of countdown, secrecy, and coming rupture.

content.fact: Dabi’s identity as Toya Todoroki must remain hidden until his public broadcast during the war.
content.injection: Before the Dabi broadcast anchor, keep Toya identity hidden. Dabi may behave with private intent, but the narrative must not expose the truth early.

entries.title: Do Not Jump To Dark Hero Before War Collapse

context.label: Hawks intelligence points heroes toward PLF war

content.fact: Deku’s solo Dark Hero period is a post-war response and must not be treated as active before the war breaks hero society.
content.injection: Before the war aftermath, keep Deku within the student/hero-operation framework. Do not make him a lone vigilante early.
